prompt: A solid substance is heated continuously. The graph shows temperature vs. time. Label the regions where energy is used for temperature increase vs. phase change, and identify what happens during the flat plateaus.
options: Solid heating (ΔT increases, PE constant)
Energy is being wasted here
No phase change, so no energy input
Temperature determines energy, not time
Melting plateau (T constant, energy → breaking bonds)
No energy is absorbed during flat sections
Flat means temperature isn't changing, so done heating
Heat only matters when temperature rises
Liquid heating (ΔT increases, PE increases slightly)
Liquid doesn't need energy to heat
Once melted, temperature automatically rises
This region is the same as the solid heating region
Boiling plateau (T constant, energy → overcoming IMF)
At boiling point, heating stops
The flat section means the substance is done changing
Boiling and melting plateaus are identical
Gas heating (ΔT increases, PE increases)
Gas doesn't require continued heating
Temperature can't rise above boiling point
Heating a gas uses no energy

hotspots.label: Melting plateau (T constant, energy → breaking bonds)
Liquid heating (ΔT increases, PE increases slightly)
Boiling plateau (T constant, energy → overcoming IMF)
